## SpaceX Starship Flight 13 fails to launch; coverage questions stock resilience amid repeated delays

Repeated Starship launch failures directly challenge the cadence thesis that underpins SPCX's long-term commercial payload revenue ramp; each delay extends the time horizon to cash-flow generation and pressures the valuation premium embedded in the private-market price.

- Starship Flight 13 did not successfully launch, marking another operational setback
- Headlines explicitly frame the question of whether SPCX stock can survive continued failures
- Launch cadence and program timeline implications are the investor concern raised
- No quantified impact on valuation or revised timeline provided in coverage
- The 'unlucky Flight 13' framing suggests this is part of a pattern, not an isolated event
